reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — desktop+mobile screenshots + conformance runner + CDN export/repo probe

rubric.evidence: conformance (webgpu, headless no-GPU): loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. worker.js dynamic-imports @huggingface/transformers@3.7.5 and runs Phi3VForCausalLM + AutoProcessor (Phi3VProcessor) in a Web Worker, streaming via TextStreamer. CDN-probe verified: the 3.7.5 bundle registers model_type 'phi3_v' → Phi3VForCausalLM in the causal-LM mapping and exports Phi3VProcessor/Phi3VImageProcessor; onnx-community/Phi-3.5-vision-instruct ships the three matching ONNX sessions (vision_encoder, prepare_inputs_embeds, model) in q4/q4f16.
rubric.notes: Genuinely runnable on the shared 3.7.5 pin — NO version-pin escape hatch needed. On a real GPU it streams a live answer; on this no-GPU runner it shows the honest needs-WebGPU state, never a faked one.

rubric.evidence: The 'See inside' panel exposes the true I/O shape: the constructed chat template (with <|image_1|> placeholders expanded), a live image-token count (the processor negates image-token ids; the worker counts them), and a token stream with per-token arrival times. io-shape-semantic=manual (needs a GPU device to confirm a real answer references the image + prompt).
rubric.notes: Expected VLM output shape (image tokens → streamed text) is explicit and legible. Semantic sanity of a real answer awaits a WebGPU device.

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id=pass (onnx-community/Phi-3.5-vision-instruct linked to its card, plus the base microsoft/ repo), declares-task=pass (image-text-to-text), declares-backend=pass, declares-quantisation=pass (q4f16), inference-off-main-thread=pass. Desktop screenshot at-a-glance shows the exact class (Phi3VForCausalLM + Phi3VProcessor, three ONNX sessions), dtype (all q4f16, needs shader-f16), backend (WebGPU required), size (~2.5 GB), license (MIT).
rubric.notes: Runtime disclosure is precise and honest about the device bar (WebGPU + shader-f16) and the ~4.2B param / ~2.5 GB download.

rubric.evidence: shared-auto-init-loader=pass (createModelLoader, no hand-rolled Load button). Desktop + wild screenshots show the loader detail line 'onnx-community/Phi-3.5-vision-instruct · transformers.js · ~2500 MB · 0 MB cached of 10737 MB' — accurate cache accounting, no auto-download on a fresh profile.
rubric.notes: auto-init-on-cached (warm-cache returning user) still to be exercised on a GPU device; the state machine and clear-cache control come from the shared loader.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass, webgpu-capability-honest=blocked. Screenshots (main + wild) show the exact honest fallback: "This model can't run on this device/browser. It needs WebGPU (no GPU adapter here). Enable it via chrome://gpu / a WebGPU-capable browser." honest-progress-retry-offline=manual.
rubric.notes: Textbook honest capability handling — a labelled needs-WebGPU explanation with enable steps, no blank panel, no faked output. blocked (not fail) is the correct honest outcome on a no-GPU runner.

rubric.evidence: Real controls across the ladder: sample-image strip, drop/upload zone(s) (role=button, aria-label), prompt input, quick-prompt chips, max-tokens number input, Generate/Answer/Compare buttons, copy buttons, and the wild page's two per-image file inputs + preset-pair radio-like chips (aria-pressed). controls-operable=manual. All action buttons are disabled until the model is ready (honest on a no-GPU device).
rubric.notes: Chips are ≥36px and the dropzone ≥44px per the tap-target guidance; a full keyboard/focus pass on the enabled controls belongs on a WebGPU device.

rubric.evidence: no-fake-output=manual; screenshots confirm placeholders only ('The model's answer will stream here.', 'The comparison streams here.') with the needs-WebGPU state shown — nothing is fabricated as model output. The readout (image tokens / tokens / time / tok/s) stays em-dash until a real run populates it.
rubric.notes: Confirmed no canned answer on any of the five pages.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt=pass (sample + preview + multi-slot images have descriptive alt), buttons-have-names=pass. Status/answer regions use role=status / aria-live=polite; the wild multi-image slots and preset-pair fieldset use a legend + aria-pressed chips.
rubric.notes: Strong. Screen-reader wording of the streaming token panel + the two-slot compare UI could be reviewed on a GPU device where controls enable.

rubric.evidence: conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass. responsive-check matrix: desktop (1280×800) clean, mobile (360×740 DPR3) clean — no horizontal overflow, no clipped controls, console + network clean on BOTH classes. Screenshots show the two-column image/output grid reflowing to a single column on narrow viewports. parity-both-classes=manual.
rubric.notes: Layout parity good on both classes. Because the interactive path is WebGPU-gated, a full mobile interaction pass needs a mobile GPU device; the unsupported-state layout is clean on both. No sub-24px tap-target notes were raised (chips/dropzones sized ≥36–44px).

rubric.evidence: All generation runs in a Web Worker with token streaming; the 'See inside' panel surfaces per-token timing and tok/s (a built-in latency readout). inp-stays-low=manual.
rubric.notes: Streaming + off-thread generation is INP-friendly by construction (guidance 'performance': offload >250ms work to a Web Worker). Measure tok/s + INP on a real GPU; Phi-3.5-vision's HD transform makes prefill heavier than a small VLM, so first-token latency is worth watching.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: INP/main-thread: offload work >250ms to a Web Worker, keep UI updates synchronous and light, break up long tasks / yield.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all Phi-3.5-vision inference (load + generate) runs in models/phi-3.5-vision/worker.js; the page only appends streamed tokens to the answer region. No blocking loop on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; worker.js TextStreamer callback posts tokens to the page

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use intrinsic-sizing flexbox with flex-wrap and min-inline-size:0 so a multi-column control panel reflows to one column on narrow viewports without horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .vlm-grid is flex-wrap with flex-basis columns and min-inline-size:0 on the output column; the wild page's two image slots and dual dropzones also wrap. Verified no overflow at 360px.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean + mobile clean (no horizontal overflow); PHI_CSS in phi3v.js

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce dynamic loading/error status in a polite live region; give every control an accessible name and every content image alt text.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — status + answer use role=status aria-live=polite (via the shared loader and page); images-have-alt and buttons-have-names both pass; the honest error status uses the loader's .err live region.

guidanceConsulted.query: feature detect WebGPU capability show honest unsupported fallback state
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Detect the capability with a real adapter probe and show a labelled unsupported state with enable steps — never a blank panel or faked output.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — createModelLoader (and the worker probe) call navigator.gpu.requestAdapter(); no adapter ⇒ labelled needs-WebGPU message with chrome://gpu steps, controls stay gated.

openQuestions: On a WebGPU device: does Phi-3.5-vision produce a coherent answer that actually references the given image + prompt, streaming token by token, and does it read the chart values correctly?
Does the multi-image compare path produce matching image-token counts for both frames after the common-square resize, and does the model attend to both images (image 1 vs image 2)?
Is the demo usable on a mobile WebGPU device given the ~2.5 GB download + ~4.2B params, or should mobile be recorded unsupported with a memory-requirement evidence note?
Do the disabled controls expose an accessible explanation of WHY they're disabled (tied to the needs-WebGPU status)?

followUpGoals.goal: GPU-device matrix pass for phi-3.5-vision: confirm streaming output + chart-value correctness + multi-image compare on desktop WebGPU; assess mobile WebGPU memory feasibility (~2.5 GB / ~4.2B), then set support (ok, or mobile:unsupported+memory-evidence).
Add a Phi-3.5-vision multi-model 'chart Q&A → speak the answer' demo composing it with a browser TTS model (e.g. Kokoro), distinct from the read-then-summarise multi-model page.
Add a 'wild' 3+ image contact-sheet demo (num_crops-aware) if larger multi-image turns stay within browser memory on a GPU device.

summary: phi-3.5-vision is a genuine in-browser multi-frame VLM (Microsoft Phi-3.5-vision, onnx-community ONNX build) that runs on the shared Transformers.js 3.7.5 pin — no version-pin escape hatch needed: the bundle registers phi3_v → Phi3VForCausalLM and exports Phi3VProcessor, and the repo ships the three matching q4f16 ONNX sessions. Distinct from the built Qwen2-VL/SmolVLM/PaliGemma demos by its HD-transform OCR/chart reasoning and genuine multi-image comparison (wild page). Honest capability handling is exemplary: on a no-GPU runner every page shows a labelled needs-WebGPU explanation with enable steps and accurate cache accounting (0 MB cached of 10737 MB), never a faked answer — webgpu-capability-honest is correctly blocked, not failed. Runtime disclosure, accessibility, and mobile+desktop layout parity are clean (desktop + mobile automated-clean, no overflow). Because the interactive path is WebGPU-gated, the semantic-output + full-interaction checks and a mobile-WebGPU memory-feasibility call need a GPU device before support flips from needs-review. Revision 2, hand-reviewed from screenshots + runner evidence + CDN/repo probe.
